head.hi: Madame Chiang Kai-shek to Mr. Lauchlin Currie, Administrative Assistant to President Roosevelt
head.note: Copy of telegram transmitted to President Roosevelt by Mr. Currie. Photostatic copy obtained from the Franklin D. Roosevelt Library, Hyde Park, N. Y.

div.p: Although Generalissimo repeatedly made offer through DennysMaj. Gen. Lancelot Ernest Dennys, British Military Attaché in China. and later WavellGen. Sir Archibald P. Wavell, British Commander in Chief, India. to send two Chinese army corps reinforce Burma force offer was declined on grounds that Burma only needs three regiments. These regiments now sent to Keng-tung while offered army corps despatched China fronts.
